summary.intent: Add CLI-based GitLab MR approval rule management, then automate it for ocp-build-data shipment MRs by reading an mr_approvers field from group.yml and configuring approval rules (keeping ART, removing others, adding new groups) when the release_from_fbc pipeline creates MRs.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented full automation: added mr_approvers to group.yml schema, created set_mr_approval_rules() in GitLabClient, integrated it into release_from_fbc pipeline with dry-run support and error handling, fixed two review-identified bugs (dry-run API calls and dict validation), and added 17 unit tests covering all new code paths. All 35 tests pass.

repo: GitLab MR-level approval rules: DELETE on project-inherited rules only removes the override, doesn't truly delete (rules reappear from project defaults)
ocp-build-data assembly_group_config.schema.json has additionalProperties:false requiring explicit allowlist for new fields
release_from_fbc pipeline reads group.yml via doozer --group=X config:read-group field_name
ART approval group is a project-level default that must always be preserved when manipulating approval rules
python-gitlab library has native MR approval rule support (mr.approval_rules.list/create, rule.delete) - no raw HTTP needed

code.finding: dry_run check was after get_mr_from_url(), causing API failures with placeholder URLs during dry-run - should check dry_run before any API calls
stdlib_yaml.safe_load can return non-dict types (string, list, int, None) - must validate isinstance(result, dict) before returning to callers expecting dict
Must check self.dry_run before calling set_mr_approval_rules to avoid API resolution of placeholder mr_url during dry-run mode

workflow: glab api fails with array parameters (-f 'user_ids[]=X') due to encoding issues - curl with JSON body is more reliable for complex payloads
glab authenticates via GITLAB_TOKEN env var and supports --hostname for targeting non-default GitLab instances
GitLab API returns 204 No Content (empty body) on successful DELETE
Pre-commit hooks can fail in sandbox mode - run git commit outside sandbox if blocked
unittest async testing: use AsyncMock for async methods, MagicMock for sync, asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete() to execute

friction: glab api --method POST with -f 'user_ids[]=15399' failed due to array encoding issue - had to use curl with raw JSON instead
Ask mode blocked initial test writing attempt
Pre-commit hook failed in sandbox, required re-run with dangerouslyDisableSandbox
Test failure: MagicMock vs AsyncMock mismatch for shipment_data_repo (async methods need AsyncMock)
GitLab API behavior not obvious: DELETE on inherited rules doesn't remove them, just undoes override
open_items: Add schema constraints for mr_approvers: patternProperties mapping group names to arrays of usernames (currently unconstrained object)
Roll out mr_approvers field to all logging-* branches (currently only logging-6.5 for testing)
Document fallback behavior when all usernames fail to resolve (skips rule, keeps project defaults)
